label: yes
cot_prefix: Okay, let me read this passage carefully. The passage starts by talking about how both men and women are surrounded by media that focuses on dieting, exercise, fashion, and hairstyles. It mentions that for men, the past idea of being smart and handsome by just wearing a nice suit is gone. Then it references a survey by the University of the West of England (UWE) that found 80% of men are unhappy with their bodies, specifically mentioning beer bellies and lack of muscles. The next part discusses the idea that this change might be due to the influence of role models seen on TV and in magazines, which set a standard for the perfect body. It also mentions a product, a muscle-enhancing shirt, which is criticized as a gimmick by Joe Warner, who argues people should exercise instead of taking shortcuts. The passage then touches on the importance of character over appearance and ends with the author considering going to the gym to improve health and self-esteem. The question is asking what the passage mainly talks about, with four options provided. The options are: A) You shouldn't judge a book by its cover. B) More and more people become obese in England. C) Role models put great pressure on common people. D) Men become more and more conscious of their body image. I need to determine which of these options best summarizes the main topic of the passage. First, option A is a proverb about not judging by appearance, which is mentioned near the end, but the passage is more about how men are concerned with their body image, not about the general principle of not judging by appearance. So A might not be the main focus. Option B talks about obesity in England. The passage does mention obesity and overweight people, but it's not the main focus. The survey mentions 80% of men being unhappy with their bodies, but the passage also discusses the reasons (influence of media, role models) and solutions (exercise, gym), so B is too narrow. Option C is about role models putting pressure on people. The passage does mention that role models in media influence people's perception of the perfect body, but this is one part of the discussion, not the main topic. The passage is broader than just the pressure from role models. Option D is about men becoming more conscious of their body image. The passage begins
